metadata.title: Make It Reliable + Prove It's Clean
metadata.playlist: Shop for Ingredients — Collect Your Madison Data
metadata.source: INFO 7375 Assignment 3 brief — Shop for Ingredients: Collect Your Madison Data

beats.narration_text: Hola — this is Liam. Episode 5 of 7. You have a saved data file. Now prove it is actually clean. The quality section is sixteen points. Eight of those points are literally the numbers — total records, clean records, percentage quality rate. If you do not document the numbers, you lose eight points for not counting. The other eight points go to data that passes the quality checklist. And the craft section is about reliability: when one source fails, does your workflow crash — or does it log the failure and keep running on the other two? Today you check the quality checklist, write down the numbers, and add an error-handling branch. Let's go.

props.segment: Make It Reliable + Prove It's Clean
props.command: Check the quality checklist. Document the numbers. Add error handling for source failures.
props.runningText: 8 of the 16 quality points are literally the numbers — count them now…
output: Quality checklist: 80%+ complete, no dupes, YYYY-MM-DD, relevant records only
Document: total records · clean records · quality rate %
Error handling: log 'Source unavailable', keep the other two running

beats.narration_text: The quality checklist has five items. One: completeness — at least eighty percent of your records should have all essential fields populated. Essential fields are title, date, and source. A record missing any of these three cannot be usefully sorted, identified, or traced. Two: no duplicates — you ran the dedupe step in Episode 4. Verify it worked by scanning for repeated titles or URLs. Three: consistent dates — every date field must be in YYYY-MM-DD. Open the file and sort by date. If some dates sort correctly and others do not, the format is inconsistent and the standardization step did not fully run. Four: relevance — every record should be about the problem from your A2. If you have records about unrelated topics that slipped through because the RSS feed was broad, remove them. Five: record count — target is fifty to two hundred clean records. Excellence is one hundred fifty to three hundred. The upper bound is not higher — the question is how clean and relevant, not how many.

artifactLines: 1. Completeness: ≥80% of records have title + date + source populated.
2. No duplicates: verify dedupe worked — scan for repeated titles or URLs.
3. Consistent dates: all in YYYY-MM-DD. Sort by date — misformatted dates sort wrong.
4. Relevance: every record is about the A2 problem. Remove off-topic records.
5. Count: 50–200 clean records. Excellence: 150–300. Quality beats quantity.
props.sparkLine: Completeness. No dupes. YYYY-MM-DD. Relevant. Right count.

beats.narration_text: Eight of the sixteen quality points are literally the numbers. Write them down now, before you write any documentation. Total records pulled: how many rows came out of the three source nodes before cleanup. Clean records after cleanup: how many rows are in the saved file after deduplication and dropping incompletes. Quality rate: clean divided by total, expressed as a percentage. Sources: the name and URL of each source you used. Status line: 'ready for Assignment 4.' These five numbers and one status line are what the grader uses to verify your quality section. If they are not in your documentation, you lose the eight points that correspond to them — not because your data is bad, but because you did not count. Count now, while the workflow output is in front of you. Write the numbers in a notes file so you have them for the documentation step in Episode 6.

props.artifactHeading: Quality numbers — write these down now
artifactLines: Total records pulled: rows from 3 sources before cleanup.
Clean records: rows in the saved file after dedupe + drop incompletes.
Quality rate: clean ÷ total × 100 — express as a percentage.
Sources: name + URL for each of the 3 sources.
Status: 'ready for Assignment 4'
Rule: 8 of 16 quality points are these numbers. Not documented = not graded.
props.sparkLine: Count now. Write it down. 8 points depend on it.

beats.act: error handling — when a source fails
beats.narration_text: Craft excellence comes from reliability. What happens when one of your three sources is unavailable — the RSS feed is down, the API returns a timeout, or the CSV path is wrong? A workflow that crashes stops. A workflow with error handling logs the failure, marks that source as unavailable, and continues on the other two. In n8n, you add an Error Trigger or an If node after each source node. If the source returns an error, route the execution to a branch that logs the message 'Source unavailable: [source name]' and passes an empty list to the Cleanup node. The Cleanup node already handles merging — if one of its three inputs is an empty list, it just merges fewer records. This is not a fallback that makes the data worse. It is a professional implementation detail that tells the grader: this workflow runs under real conditions, not only under perfect ones.

props.artifactHeading: Craft excellence — when a source fails
artifactLines: Without error handling: one bad source crashes the entire workflow.
With error handling: log 'Source unavailable: [name]', pass empty list to Cleanup, keep running.
Add an If or Error Trigger node after each source node — route failures to the log branch.
Cleanup merges whatever it receives — empty input from one source = fewer records, not a crash.
This is the Kanishk example: reliability beats complexity.
props.sparkLine: Log the failure. Keep running on the other two.

beats.narration_text: Verdict for Make It Reliable and Prove It's Clean. The quality checklist: eighty percent or more completeness, no duplicates, all dates in YYYY-MM-DD, every record relevant to the A2 problem, fifty to two hundred records. Write down five numbers now: total records pulled, clean records after cleanup, quality rate as a percentage, source names, and status ready for Assignment 4. Eight of the sixteen quality points are those numbers — not documented means not graded. Craft excellence is error handling: one If or Error Trigger node after each source routes failures to a log message and passes an empty list to Cleanup. The workflow runs on the other two sources. Reliability beats complexity. Reliability is what separates a working prototype from a demo that only works in a clean room.

props.artifactTitle: Make It Reliable + Prove It's Clean
artifactLines: Checklist: ≥80% complete · no dupes · YYYY-MM-DD · relevant · 50–200 records.
Numbers to write down: total pulled · clean records · quality rate % · sources · status.
8 of 16 quality points = those numbers. Not written = not graded.
Error handling: If/Error Trigger after each source → log 'Source unavailable' → empty list to Cleanup.
Reliability over complexity: a workflow that runs on 2 sources beats one that crashes on 3.

beats.narration_text: Your turn. Paste this prompt into Claude to review a sample of your records and report the quality numbers. The prompt reads: I have a data file for an n8n workflow. Here is a sample of ten rows: [paste ten rows from your CSV or JSON]. The required fields are: title, date in YYYY-MM-DD, and source name. Report: how many of these ten records have all three required fields, the percentage completeness for this sample, any duplicate titles or URLs, any dates not in YYYY-MM-DD format, and any records that appear to be off-topic for this problem: [A2 problem in one sentence]. Based on the sample, estimate whether the full dataset is likely to pass the eighty percent completeness threshold. That prompt takes ten rows — not all two hundred — and gives you a credible quality estimate in under thirty seconds. Run it. If the sample fails, fix the cleanup step before you document the quality numbers.

props.segment: Make It Reliable + Prove It's Clean
props.command: Here is a sample of 10 rows from my data: [paste 10 rows]. Required fields: title, date (YYYY-MM-DD), source name. Report: completeness %, duplicate titles/URLs, dates not in YYYY-MM-DD, off-topic records. A2 problem: [one sentence]. Estimate if the full dataset passes the 80% completeness threshold.
props.runningText: 10 rows is enough for a quality estimate — fix cleanup first if the sample fails…

beats.narration_text: Make It Reliable + Prove It's Clean. Liam, in for Bear.
